name: rmf_traffic_msgs
description: A package containing messages used by the RMF traffic management system.

BlockadeCancel.description: The participant whose reservation is being canceled
BlockadeCancel.raw:
# The participant whose reservation is being canceled
uint64 participant

# True if all reservations for this participants should be considered cancelled
bool all_reservations

# If all_reservations is false, then this is the last reservation that should
# be considered cancelled. If all_reservations is true, then this field is
# meaningless
uint64 reservation

BlockadeCheckpoint.raw:
# The position of the checkpoint
float64[2] position

# The name of the map that the checkpoint is on
string map_name

# Whether or not the participant can hold at this checkpoint
bool can_hold

BlockadeHeartbeat.description: An array of the current blockade statuses which describe the most recent
BlockadeHeartbeat.raw:
# An array of the current blockade statuses which describe the most recent
# information
BlockadeStatus[] statuses

# This will be true when the blockade moderator has identified a gridlock that
# cannot be undone. This should never happen if a system is setup correctly. But
# it may happen if a robot is given a path whose first or last checkpoint is in
# conflict with the path of another robot.
bool has_gridlock

BlockadeReached.description: The ID of the blockade participant
BlockadeReached.raw:
# The ID of the blockade participant
uint64 participant

# The reservation ID that this update refers to
uint64 reservation

# The checkpoint that has been reached
uint64 checkpoint

BlockadeReady.description: The ID of the blockade participant
BlockadeReady.raw:
# The ID of the blockade participant
uint64 participant

# The reservation ID that this update refers to
uint64 reservation

# The checkpoint that is ready to be left
uint64 checkpoint

BlockadeRelease.description: The ID of the blockade participant
BlockadeRelease.raw:
# The ID of the blockade participant
uint64 participant

# The reservation ID that this update refers to
uint64 reservation

# The checkpoint that should be released
uint64 checkpoint

BlockadeSet.description: The ID of the participant that is setting its path
BlockadeSet.raw:
# The ID of the participant that is setting its path
uint64 participant

# The ID of the reservation that is being set
uint64 reservation

# The radius to inflate the path
float64 radius

# The path that is being reserved
BlockadeCheckpoint[] path

BlockadeStatus.description: The Participant ID that this status is for
BlockadeStatus.raw:
# The Participant ID that this status is for
uint64 participant

# The latest reservation known for this participant
uint64 reservation

# This is true if and only if the moderator has ever received a ready notice
# from the participant
bool any_ready

# If any_ready is true, then this is the most recent ready checkpoint that the
# moderator knows about. If any_ready is false, then this field is meaningless.
uint64 last_ready

# The last checkpoint that the moderator knows of the participant reaching
uint64 last_reached

# The first checkpoint that's currently blockaded for this participant
uint64 assignment_begin

# The last checkpoint that's currently blockaded for this participant
uint64 assignment_end

Circle.description: The radius of the circle. The circle will be centered around the origin of its
Circle.raw:
# The radius of the circle. The circle will be centered around the origin of its
# frame of reference.
float64 radius

ConvexShape.description: A 2D box or circle shape reference.
ConvexShape.raw:
# A 2D box or circle shape reference.

uint8 NONE=0
uint8 BOX=1
uint8 CIRCLE=2

# Choose between the BOX and CIRCLE types
uint8 type

# Specify the index of the shape. We support 256 different convex shapes per
# context.
uint8 index

ConvexShapeContext.description: Circle descriptions which can be used by the ConvexShape message
ConvexShapeContext.raw:
# Circle descriptions which can be used by the ConvexShape message
Circle[] circles

ItineraryDelay.raw:
uint64 participant

int64 delay

uint64 itinerary_version

ItineraryErase.raw:
uint64 participant

uint64[] routes

uint64 itinerary_version

ItineraryExtend.description: ID of the schedule participant whose itinerary is being extended
ItineraryExtend.raw:
# ID of the schedule participant whose itinerary is being extended
uint64 participant

# The new routes that are being added to the itinerary
Route[] routes

uint64 itinerary_version

ItineraryReached.raw:
uint64 participant
uint64 plan
uint64[] reached_checkpoints
uint64 progress_version

ItinerarySet.description: ID of the schedule participant whose itinerary is being set
ItinerarySet.raw:
# ID of the schedule participant whose itinerary is being set
uint64 participant

# ID of this plan
uint64 plan

# The new itinerary for this participant
Route[] itinerary

# The storage location for these routes
uint64 storage_base

uint64 itinerary_version

MirrorUpdate.description: The version of the schedule node that provided this update
MirrorUpdate.raw:
# The version of the schedule node that provided this update
ScheduleIdentity node_id

# The version of the database this update provides
uint64 database_version

# The patch for the query
SchedulePatch patch

# True if this update is meant to remedy a mirror that has fallen
# out of sync
bool is_remedial_update

NegotiationAck.description: The version number of the conflict whose conclusion is being acknowledged
NegotiationAck.raw:
# The version number of the conflict whose conclusion is being acknowledged
uint64 conflict_version

# The participants who are acknowledging the conclusion of the conflict
# negotiation
NegotiationParticipantAck[] acknowledgments

NegotiationConclusion.description: The version number assigned to this conflict
NegotiationConclusion.raw:
# The version number assigned to this conflict
uint64 conflict_version

# True if the conflict was resolved. False if the negotiation was abandoned.
bool resolved

# The ID sequence for the negotiation table that was selected
NegotiationKey[] table

NegotiationForfeit.description: The conflict ID that this forfeit is targeted at
NegotiationForfeit.raw:
# The conflict ID that this forfeit is targeted at
uint64 conflict_version

# Forfeit this negotiation table
NegotiationKey[] table

NegotiationKey.description: The participant ID of the negotiation table
NegotiationKey.raw:
# The participant ID of the negotiation table
uint64 participant

# The version of the negotiation table that we care about
uint64 version

NegotiationNotice.description: The version number assigned to this conflict
NegotiationNotice.raw:
# The version number assigned to this conflict
uint64 conflict_version

# The IDs of the participants that are in conflict.
uint64[] participants

NegotiationParticipantAck.raw:
# The participant that is acknowledging
uint64 participant

# Whether this participant will be updating
bool updating false

# The itinerary version that will provide the update to
# conform to the negotiation result
uint64 itinerary_version

NegotiationProposal.description: The conflict ID that this proposal is targeted at
NegotiationProposal.raw:
# The conflict ID that this proposal is targeted at
uint64 conflict_version

# The version number for this proposal within the negotiation
uint64 proposal_version

# The participant ID that this proposal is coming from
uint64 for_participant

# The participant IDs that this proposal is trying to accommodate. As each
# participant proposes their ideal itinerary, the other participants in the
# conflict will propose itineraries which accommodate it.
#
# The order of IDs in this dynamic array have important semantic meaning about
# which itineraries are being accommodated. For example:
#
# [] (empty):  This proposal does not accommodate any other participants. This
#              is the best possible itinerary for this participant.
#
# [3]:         This proposal is the best itinerary that can accommodate the
#              ideal itinerary of participant 3.
#
# [3, 7]:      This proposal is the best itinerary for this participant that can
#              accommodate both the ideal itinerary of participant 3 and the
#              best itinerary of participant 7 that accommodates the ideal
#              itinerary of participant 3.
#
# [3, 7, ...]: This proposal is the best itinerary that can accommodate the
#              ideal itinerary of participant 3 and the best itineraries that
#              accommodate the best itineraries of the participants that precede
#              them in the list, recursively.
NegotiationKey[] to_accommodate

# The unique ID for the plan that is being proposed
uint64 plan_id

# The itinerary that is being proposed for this participant
Route[] itinerary

NegotiationRefusal.description: The ID of the conflict negotiation that is being refused
NegotiationRefusal.raw:
# The ID of the conflict negotiation that is being refused
uint64 conflict_version

NegotiationRejection.description: The conflict ID that this rejection is targeted at
NegotiationRejection.raw:
# The conflict ID that this rejection is targeted at
uint64 conflict_version

# Reject this negotiation table
NegotiationKey[] table

# The rejection is by this participant
uint64 rejected_by

Itinerary[] alternatives

NegotiationRepeat.description: Repeat conflict information related to this version
NegotiationRepeat.raw:
# Repeat conflict information related to this version
uint64 conflict_version

# Repeat conflict information related to this table. If this is empty, then
# only the initial NegotiationNotice will be repeated.
uint64[] table

NegotiationState.raw:
NegotiationStatus status
NegotiationTreeNode[] tree
NegotiationProposal[] orphan_proposals
NegotiationRejection[] orphan_rejections
NegotiationForfeit[] orphan_forfeits

NegotiationStatus.raw:
uint64 conflict_version
uint64[] participants

# Time that this negotiation began
builtin_interfaces/Time start_time

# Time that the last response from a participant was seen
builtin_interfaces/Time last_response_time

NegotiationTreeNode.raw:
int64 parent
NegotiationKey key
bool rejected
Route[] itinerary

Participant.description: The unique ID for this participant
Participant.raw:
# The unique ID for this participant
uint64 id

# Description of this participant (name, shape, etc.)
ParticipantDescription description

ParticipantDescription.raw:
# Name of the participant
string name

# Owner of the participant (e.g. fleet name)
string owner

uint8 RX_INVALID = 0
uint8 RX_UNRESPONSIVE = 1
uint8 RX_RESPONSIVE = 2

# Whether or not this participant will automatically respond to conflicts
uint8 responsiveness

# The physical profile of this Participant
Profile profile

Participants.description: The version of the schedule node that provided this update
Participants.raw:
# The version of the schedule node that provided this update
ScheduleIdentity node_id

# A list of participants with their IDs
Participant[] participants

Profile.description: The shape that specifies the footprint of a schedule participant
Profile.raw:
# The shape that specifies the footprint of a schedule participant
ConvexShape footprint

# The shape that specifies the vicinity around a schedule participant
ConvexShape vicinity

# The buffer that contains the shape descriptions
ConvexShapeContext shape_context

Region.raw:
string map

Space[] spaces

Timespan timespan

Route.raw:
string map

Trajectory trajectory

uint64[] checkpoints

TrafficDependency[] dependencies

ScheduleChangeAdd.description: The Plan ID for the new routes
ScheduleChangeAdd.raw:
# The Plan ID for the new routes
uint64 plan_id

# The new route items to add
ScheduleChangeAddItem[] items

ScheduleChangeAddItem.raw:
# The ID for this route
uint64 route_id

# The storage location for this route
uint64 storage_id

# The description of this route
Route route

ScheduleChangeProgress.description: Indicate whether any progress has actually been made. If false, then the
ScheduleChangeProgress.raw:
# Indicate whether any progress has actually been made. If false, then the
# rest of the fields can be ignored
bool has_progress

# The version of the progress within the plan
uint64 version

# The checkpoints in the itinerary that have been reached
uint64[] checkpoints

ScheduleIdentity.description: The UUID of the new schedule node
ScheduleIdentity.raw:
# The UUID of the new schedule node
# TODO(MXG): Consider using uuid_msgs here: https://github.com/ros-geographic-info/unique_identifier/blob/master/uuid_msgs/msg/UniqueID.msg
string node_uuid

# The time that the new schedule node was started. In the event that multiple
# schedule nodes have been started, the one with the newest timestamp will be
# considered the active node, and the rest of the nodes will shut down.
builtin_interfaces/Time timestamp

ScheduleInconsistency.raw:
uint64 participant

ScheduleInconsistencyRange[] ranges

uint64 last_known_itinerary

uint64 last_known_progress

ScheduleParticipantPatch.raw:
uint64 participant_id

uint64 itinerary_version

uint64[] erasures

ScheduleChangeDelay[] delays

ScheduleChangeAdd additions

ScheduleChangeProgress progress

SchedulePatch.description: The changes to the schedule, grouped into the different participants
SchedulePatch.raw:
# The changes to the schedule, grouped into the different participants
ScheduleParticipantPatch[] participants

# TODO(MXG): The database will only ever report 1 cull per update. Consider
# making this a single field instead of a dynamic array.
ScheduleChangeCull[] cull

bool has_base_version
uint64 base_version
uint64 latest_version

ScheduleQueries.description: The version of the schedule node that provided this update
ScheduleQueries.raw:
# The version of the schedule node that provided this update
ScheduleIdentity node_id

# The list of known queries
ScheduleQuery[] queries

# The list of IDs for those queries
uint64[] query_ids

ScheduleQueryParticipants.raw:
uint16 ALL=1
uint16 INCLUDE=2
uint16 EXCLUDE=3

uint16 type

uint64[] ids

ScheduleQuerySpacetime.raw:
uint16 ALL=1
uint16 REGIONS=2
uint16 TIMESPAN=3

uint16 type

# =====================
# ===== REGIONS =====
# If REGIONS mode is chosen, this will contain the regions to query
Region[] regions

ShapeContext shape_context

# =====================
# ===== TIMESPAN ======
Timespan timespan

Shape.raw:
# A 2D shape reference.

uint8 NONE=0
uint8 BOX=1
uint8 CIRCLE=2

# TODO(MXG): Add support for the SimplePolygon class
# uint8 SIMPLE_POLYGON=3

# Choose between the BOX and CIRCLE types
uint8 type

# Specify the index of the shape. We support 256 different convex shapes per
# trajectory. If more shapes are needed than that, then the trajectory must be
# split into more trajectories.
uint8 index

ShapeContext.description: The convex shape descriptions that are available
ShapeContext.raw:
# The convex shape descriptions that are available
ConvexShapeContext convex_shapes

# TODO(MXG): Implement the SimplePolygon message
# SimplePolygon[] simple_polygons

Space.raw:
# The shape of this space
Shape shape

# The pose of this space
geometry_msgs/Pose2D pose

Timespan.description: TODO(MXG): Find out if it's more efficient to use a bool+value pair, or to use
Timespan.raw:
string[] maps

# TODO(MXG): Find out if it's more efficient to use a bool+value pair, or to use
# a dynamic array of the value (which will only ever have 1 or 0 entries)

bool has_lower_bound
int64 lower_bound

bool has_upper_bound
int64 upper_bound

TrafficDependency.raw:
uint64 dependent_checkpoint

uint64 on_participant
uint64 on_plan
uint64 on_route
uint64 on_checkpoint

Trajectory.description: A Trajectory is a container of Waypoints. The standard way to interpret the
Trajectory.raw:
# A Trajectory is a container of Waypoints. The standard way to interpret the
# motion of a Trajectory is as a piecewise cubic spline connecting the
# waypoints.
TrajectoryWaypoint[] waypoints

TrajectoryWaypoint.description: The time when this Waypoint should be reached
TrajectoryWaypoint.raw:
# The time when this Waypoint should be reached
int64 time

# This is a 2D homogeneous position which mixes 2 translation coordinates (x, y)
# with 1 rotation coordinate (yaw).
#
# The position of this Waypoint
float64[3] position

# This is a 2D homogeneous screw velocity with 2 translational components (x, y)
# and 1 rotational component (yaw).
#
# The velocity that this vehicle should have when it reaches this Waypoint
float64[3] velocity

RegisterParticipant.description: The description of the participant that is being registered
RegisterParticipant.raw:
# The description of the participant that is being registered
ParticipantDescription description

---

# The ID given to the registered participant
uint64 participant_id

# The last itinerary version that this participant had
uint64 last_itinerary_version

# The last Route ID that this participant had
uint64 last_plan_id

# The next storage base for this participant to use
uint64 next_storage_base

# A string to notify about exceptional issues that came up while trying to
# fulfill the request
string error

RegisterQuery.raw:
# The query to be registered
ScheduleQuery query

---

# The identity of the schedule node that provided this registration
ScheduleIdentity node_id

# The ID given to the registered query. Use this ID when making a query request.
uint64 query_id

# A string to notify exceptional issues that came up while trying to fulfill the
# request.
string error

RequestChanges.description: Query ID to request a full participants list for
RequestChanges.raw:
# Query ID to request a full participants list for
uint64 query_id

# Version to request changes from; ignored if full_update is true
uint64 version

# Request a full update rather than from a specific version
bool full_update false

---

# Response to the request
ScheduleIdentity node_id
uint8 REQUEST_ACCEPTED=1
uint8 UNKNOWN_QUERY_ID=2
uint8 ERROR=3
uint8 result
string error

UnregisterParticipant.description: Confirmation that the participant was unregistered
UnregisterParticipant.raw:
uint64 participant_id

---

# Confirmation that the participant was unregistered
bool confirmation


# A description of any errors that were encountered, such as the participant_id
# being unknown
string error
